pattern: Model each day's currency conversions as a tree of rates rooted at the starting currency, then combine both trees only through the currencies they both reach.
hints: Since each day's conversions never contradict each other, the currencies and rates for a single day always form a tree, not a general graph -- so there is only one way to get from the starting currency to any other reachable currency on that day.
Compute, for every currency reachable on day 1, the single multiplier that takes your starting currency to it, then separately compute the same kind of multiplier for day 2.

For every currency reached on day 1, check whether day 2 can also reach it -- if so you can return home through that currency using day 2's inverse rate, so compare (day-1 multiplier) divided by (day-2 multiplier) across every shared currency, and keep the best, treating staying in the starting currency the whole time (multiplier 1) as a valid option too.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two-hop day 1 chain and three-hop day 2 chain
statement example, converting out and back through the same pair
statement example, no conversion is ever worthwhile
minimal one-letter currencies, a single pair on each day
day 2's graph is entirely disconnected from the starting currency, so no conversion beats staying put
a long day-1 chain combined with a direct day-2 shortcut back
the only reachable shared currency is a net loss, so the best answer is to never convert at all

stress.description: random forests of two-letter currencies for each day, rates spanning the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'
    pool = set()
    while len(pool) < 24:
        pool.add(''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(2)))
    pool = list(pool)
    initial = pool[0]
    def build(rest):
        rng.shuffle(rest)
        n = rng.randint(1, min(10, len(rest)))
        chosen = rest[:n]
        pairs, rates = [], []
        connected = [initial]
        for c in chosen:
            parent = rng.choice(connected)
            pairs.append([parent, c])
            rates.append(round(rng.uniform(1.0, 10.0), 4))
            connected.append(c)
        return pairs, rates
    rest = pool[1:]
    pairs1, rates1 = build(list(rest))
    pairs2, rates2 = build(list(rest))
    return [initial, pairs1, rates1, pairs2, rates2]
stress.note: each day's pairs always form a tree rooted at the initial currency, matching the problem's no-contradiction guarantee

complexity.time: O(n + m) to build and traverse each day's currency tree
complexity.space: O(n + m) for the adjacency lists and multiplier maps
